key_insight: Fine-tuning a foundation ML interatomic potential can recover the barocaloric phase transformation of ammonium sulfate with only 5–10 DFT configurations, whereas training from scratch needs more data and the base model alone fails.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical route to data-efficient, near-DFT atomistic screening of barocaloric materials, reducing the cost of discovering solid-state cooling candidates.
method: Train MACE-based MLIPs on ammonium sulfate using three strategies: from scratch, naive replay fine-tuning, and multihead replay fine-tuning.
Test each model in molecular dynamics across multiple training-set sizes for its ability to reproduce the temperature-driven structural phase transition.
Compare performance at both standard DFT and hybrid-DFT levels, including the impact of dispersion correction.
evidence_signal: Across evaluated training-set sizes, fine-tuned models reproduced the phase transformation with as few as 5–10 60-atom DFT configurations, while scratch-trained models broke down for small datasets and the foundation model missed the transition.
watch_item: The result is system-specific and appears sensitive to including dispersion; extrapolation to other materials or phase-change mechanisms may require additional validation.
paper_title: Barocaloric phase transformation from data efficient fine-tuning of machine learned interatomic potentials
